_comment: Optimized for Opus 4.7 + 1M context: PostToolUse fires per file edit, runs ruff (format + check) on ONLY the file just edited, surfaces output back into the AI session within ~200ms. The shell command is defensive: if CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS isn't populated (older Claude Code versions or non-file tool calls), it exits 0 without running ruff so the hook never blocks tool use. The `|| true` suffix ensures a non-zero ruff exit (lint errors) is surfaced as context but does not abort the in-flight tool use.

hooks.command: if [ -n "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" ]; then echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -E '\.py$' | xargs -r python -m ruff format 2>/dev/null; echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -E '\.py$' | xargs -r python -m ruff check 2>&1 | tail -20 || true; fi
